summary: CHIRP CSV Repeater Import App — allows users to upload a CHIRP CSV export file via browser, parse and save all repeater data to a backend canister for persistent storage, view and search the repeater list, and manage repeaters with Internet Identity login.
architectural_notes: Backend uses Internet Computer canister for persistent repeater storage
CHIRP CSV format must be parsed and stored in canister on import
known_issues: Previously only 17 of 4000+ imported repeaters were displaying — root cause: data not persisted to canister

existing_features.title: Update the CHIRP CSV parser (`frontend/src/utils/csvParser.ts`) to correctly map all CHIRP CSV columns — including Location, Name, Frequency, Duplex, Offset, Tone, rToneFreq, cToneFreq, DtcsCode, Mode, and Comment — into the backend Repeater type. Ensure CTCSS/PL tone fields are always normalized (e.g., '100.0' → 1000 or the appropriate integer representation the backend expects) and that city/state extraction handles all 50 US states reliably.

Update the Admin CSV import page (`frontend/src/pages/AdminPage.tsx`) so that after parsing the CHIRP CSV file, all parsed repeaters are sent to the backend `bulkAddRepeaters` canister function in batches (e.g., 200 records per batch) with progress feedback shown to the user. After all batches complete, display the total number of repeaters successfully saved, the number skipped, and any error details.

Ensure the repeater directory page (`frontend/src/pages/DirectoryPage.tsx`) correctly loads and displays all stored repeaters from the canister, including those imported via CSV. Fix any pagination, infinite scroll, or query limit issues that could cause only a subset (e.g., 17) of stored repeaters to be displayed. The page should show the full count and allow filtering/searching across all stored repeaters.

Require Internet Identity authentication to access the CSV import functionality. Users who are not logged in should see a login prompt instead of the import UI. Authenticated admins should be able to trigger bulk imports, while unauthenticated or non-admin users cannot.

new_feature_requests.summary: Browser-based CHIRP CSV file upload to import repeater data
Persistent canister storage for all imported repeaters (4000+)
View and search the saved repeater list
Internet Identity login required to save and manage repeaters

Expand the backend canister storage to support bulk insertion of 4,000+ repeaters in a single operation. Add or update a `bulkAddRepeaters` function in the main actor that accepts an array of Repeater records and stores all of them persistently in a stable data structure (e.g., TrieMap or HashMap keyed by a unique ID). Ensure no records are silently dropped or truncated during batch inserts.
